body:
## Summary

This PR changes the default value of `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` from `false` to `true` for both `solve` and `hive` commands, improving user experience when hitting API rate limits.

## Changes Made

### Configuration Files
- ✅ Updated `src/solve.config.lib.mjs`: changed default from `false` to `true`
- ✅ Updated `src/hive.config.lib.mjs`: changed default from `false` to `true`

### Documentation
- ✅ Updated `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`: changed default values in both solve and hive options tables
- ✅ Updated `README.md`: removed redundant `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` flags from example commands (since it's now default)

### Changeset
- ✅ Added changeset file `.changeset/fine-shrimps-take.md` describing the change
- ✅ Version will be automatically bumped by CI/CD using the changeset system

### Merge Resolution
- ✅ Merged main branch to resolve conflicts
- ✅ Incorporated typo fix: `--auto-restart-until-mergable` → `--auto-restart-until-mergeable`
- ✅ Preserved our change: `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` default to `true`

## Rationale

The `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` feature automatically resumes sessions when API rate limits reset, calculating the reset time and waiting appropriately. This eliminates the need for manual intervention and provides a better user experience.

By making this the default behavior:
- Users benefit from automatic session continuation without needing to know about the flag
- Reduces friction and manual work when hitting rate limits
- Maintains session context (unlike `--auto-restart-on-limit-reset` which starts fresh)
- Users who don't want this behavior can still explicitly disable it with `--no-auto-resume-on-limit-reset`

## Testing

All existing tests pass successfully (291 tests total):
- ✅ 70 queue tests
- ✅ 30 limits display tests
- ✅ 67 usage limit detection tests
- ✅ 51 Telegram message filter tests
- ✅ 22 solve_queue command tests
- ✅ 10 queue display tests
- ✅ 41 Telegram bot launcher tests

The test suite for `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` (`tests/test-auto-resume-limit-reset.mjs`) tests the behavior logic, not the default value, so no test changes were needed.

## Migration

This is a **non-breaking change** in the sense that:
- Existing scripts that explicitly set `--auto-resume-on-limit-reset` will continue to work
- Existing scripts that explicitly set `--no-auto-resume-on-limit-reset` will continue to work
- Scripts that relied on the old default behavior can add `--no-auto-resume-on-limit-reset` to preserve the old behavior

## Fixes

Fixes #1302

title: Make --auto-resume-on-limit-reset enabled by default
